what_it_does: A self-contained interactive viewer and lightweight editor for the ConceptV project: it loads a system-map.json model and renders every folder/file as nested boxes (treemap), a radial sunburst, role-based districts, or dependency layers, while drawing resolution/loads edges and supporting search, color/size lenses, selection tracing, and queued structural edits.
observations: Single HTML file with inline CSS and a large IIFE script.
Loads data from system-map.json and external styles/scripts (colors_and_type.css, cv-icons.js, cv-vi-glyph.js, cv-shapes.js, cv-glyphics.js).
Implements squarified treemap, sunburst, districts-by-role, and dependency-layer layouts.
Maintains registries for layouts (LAYOUTS), size metrics (SIZERS), color modes (COLORERS), and edge types (EDGE_TYPES).
Provides a detail panel with file descriptions, provided globals, inbound/outbound resolution links.
Has an edit mode with create, rename, duplicate, move (drag/drop or click), archive, delete, plus undo/redo and an exported operation queue.
Persists edited model and queued ops in localStorage; warns that disk writes need an external adapter.
Builds its own toolbar buttons as composed CV_GLYPHIC glyphics.
Uses hardcoded DESC, SYS, ROLE, and RC maps to annotate files and assign colors.
Auto-rescans system-map.json every 6 seconds but skips when local edits are pending.
standouts: Multi-projection map of the same directory/dependency model (nested, sunburst, districts, layers).
In-browser structural editor that emits a typed operation queue instead of touching files directly.
Toolbar icons are rendered by the same glyphic composition system the map documents.
FLIP-style morph transitions when switching lenses or applying edits.
conventions: Single-file app mixing markup, styles, and behavior.
Older JS style: var, IIFE, function expressions, manual DOM building.
Data-driven registries for projections, metrics, colorers, and edge types.
Dataset attributes bind DOM elements to model nodes.
CSS variables expected from an external stylesheet.
concerns: Depends on globals like window.CV_GLYPHIC with only a text fallback.
localStorage can diverge from disk; auto-rescan is disabled while edits exist, so stale state can persist.
Path/id collisions in rename/move are checked but rely on simple string equality.
Browser prompts for rename/new-file names are a brittle UI.
Hardcoded description and role maps must be kept in sync with the actual file tree.
notes: A one-screen, multi-lens cartography of the codebase that also doubles as a safe sandbox for proposing structural changes before an external adapter applies them.
questions: What is the exact schema of system-map.json and how is it generated?
How does the disk-write adapter handle operation ordering and conflicts?
Where is the encoding profile from CV_MEANING baked, and what happens if it is missing?
Are the icon scripts guaranteed to load before the toolbar is built?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the dsCard says it shows the whole system as nested boxes with resolution wiring; the file does that and adds lenses, search, selection tracing, and an edit mode.
apparent_themes: Multi-lens projection of a single underlying model.
Compositional design (the toolbar is made of the same glyphics it documents).
Preview model kept separate from disk truth.
intention_signals: To make the structure of the project immediately legible as a spatial map and to let a user experiment with reorganizing files without immediately altering the filesystem.
novelty: It merges directory treemap, radial sunburst, role districts, dependency layers, and an in-browser structural editor with an operation-export queue into one file, while rendering its own controls from the glyphic system it maps.
contribution: A self-contained interactive surface that turns a static directory-and-dependency model into an explorable, multi-lens map and provides a queued, reversible way to propose structural changes.
summary_for_embedding: system/system-map.html is a single-file interactive ConceptV system-map viewer and editor that renders project directories and resolution relationships via treemap/sunburst/district/layer projections, supports search, color/size lenses, selection tracing, and queued structural edits with undo/redo.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a one-screen, navigable cartography of the whole codebase and a safe sandbox for proposing structural changes before an external adapter applies them.
